title: Sour Flaky Olives Tagine
description: A traditional middle-eastern dish, sour flaky olives tagine is a flavorful and aromatic stew made with tender olives, flaky pastry, and a hint of sourness. This tagine is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: Roll out the flaky pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the pastry to a 9-inch round baking dish or tagine.
In a large bowl, combine the olives, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Spoon the olive mixture into the pastry-lined baking dish.
Brush the edges of the pastry with olive oil.
Fold the edges of the pastry up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape.
Bake the tagine for 35-40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is heated through.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
